description: When no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients is configured and the client MAC is not learned then the first L2VPN DHCP client packet is dropped.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what caveat is documented for DHCP Relay?
Feature Caveat: DHCP Relay - When no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients is configured and the client MAC is not learned then the first L2VPN DHCP client packet is dropped.

description: On 8325, 8325P and 8325H Switch series, iBGP split-horizon rule does not apply across address families For example, routes exchanged between VSX peers over iBGP may be re-advertised into other families, which can introduce routing loops. To prevent this behavior, route-maps (for example, using BGP communities) should be used to classify the routes and explicitly control their propagation. In particular, it is recommended to enforce policies using route-maps that restrict the advertisement of these routes into the EVPN address family.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - On 8325, 8325P and 8325H Switch series, iBGP split-horizon rule does not apply across address families For example, routes exchanged between VSX peers over iBGP may be re-advertised into other families, which can introduce routing loops. To prevent this behavior, route-maps (for example, using BGP communities) should be used to classify the routes and explicitly control their propagation. In particular, it is recommended to enforce policies using route-maps that restrict the advertisement of these routes into the EVPN address family.

description: When configuring the same BGP neighbor under both IPv4 and IPv6 address families on a router, if a maximum prefix limit is set on one address family (like IPv4), exceeding that limit can potentially impact the BGP session for the other address family (IPv6) as well.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what limitation is mentioned for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - When configuring the same BGP neighbor under both IPv4 and IPv6 address families on a router, if a maximum prefix limit is set on one address family (like IPv4), exceeding that limit can potentially impact the BGP session for the other address family (IPv6) as well.

description: If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what caveat is documented for Activate?

Feature Caveat: Activate - If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

description: Duplicate Address Detection (DAD) status indicated a duplicate for IPv6 anycast gateway configuration. As a workaround, configure an active gateway IPv6 address and SVI IPv6 address, as this can prevent DAD issues. Issuing the commands interface shut followed by interface no-shut can help in resolving the issue.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what caveat is documented for ARP?

Feature Caveat: ARP - Duplicate Address Detection (DAD) status indicated a duplicate for IPv6 anycast gateway configuration. As a workaround, configure an active gateway IPv6 address and SVI IPv6 address, as this can prevent DAD issues. description: After an issue with duplicate IPs on two VXLAN tunnel endpoints (VTEPs) is resolved, the kernel will have the ARP entry, but the output of the show arp and show evpn mac-ip commands do not show that IP. As a workaround, flap the physical interface.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what limitation is mentioned for ARP?

Feature Caveat: ARP - After an issue with duplicate IPs on two VXLAN tunnel endpoints (VTEPs) is resolved, the kernel will have the ARP entry, but the output of the show arp and show evpn mac-ip commands do not show that IP. As a workaround, flap the physical interface.

description: The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what additional caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp.

description: Symptom: The switch experiences traffic drop. Scenario: This issue can occur when NDMD learns a NULL MAC address via GARP from a rogue VM, and it is distributed via EVPN to a remote VTEP. New VMs requesting ARP are getting back the NULL MAC in a response and hence traffic drop is seen. Workaround: Issue the clear arp command on local the local VTEP to re-learn the ARP entry and distribute proper ARP entries.

description: Symptom: Ethernet-tag mappings are permitted only when the operational state of the EVPN VLAN-aware bundle is disabled. Scenario: In previous versions of AOS-CX, modifications to VLAN: Ethernet-tag mappings were permitted only when the operational state of the EVPN VLAN-aware bundle was disabled. In AOS-CX 10.17, this restriction has been relaxed-modifications are now allowed regardless of the operational state, provided that the resulting effective Ethernet tag does not conflict with any existing VLAN tag assignment. This ensures configuration flexibility while maintaining tag uniqueness and preventing forwarding conflicts.

description: Symptom: IVRL routes are treated as iBGP route in HPE-ANW switches. Scenario: This issue can occur when an iBGP route is received from a VSX peer and also from a different VRF via IVRL. Though both of the routes are iBGP routes having same AD (200), the route learned from vex-peer is preferred over IVRL. It is recommended to set higher local preference via route-map in direction for the IVRL route to be selected as best route always.

description: Symptom: An ifa-max-metadata-stack-length configuration is not working to delimit an IFA zone. Scenario: If, for example, a network admin 10-device topology and only wants IFA metadata from the first three devices, using the ifa-max-metadata-stack-length configuration to match the size of metadata for the three devices will not work. The admin will continue to see IFA metrics from all 10 devices in the IFA zone, because the ifa-max-metadata-stack-length configuration is being ignored. Workaround: Instead of relying on the ifa-max-metadata-stack-length, the customer can use the ifa-hop-limit configuration to delimit an IFA zone.

description: Symptom: When a large number of host-routes (fully qualified IPv4 routes with a /32 subnet mask or IPv6 routes with a /128 subnet mask) are installed on a switch with a matching directly connected host, some network events may result in traffic loss for those routes. Scenario: Most commonly this will occur on reboot, however it may be present when the switch is brought up as well. Linkflaps could also result in seeing this issue. Workaround: Clear the ARP cache to allow hosts to be cleared and re-entered and resolve the incorrect programming.

description: Symptom: When wireless clients roam between access points connected to the same switch, they lose connectivity and receive an APIPA address. Connectivity is only restored after the MAC-age timer expires. Scenario: When a client is authenticated with a VLAN on a secure port, the VLAN is added as an ingress-only VLAN on other secure ports, but the MSTP state for those ports remains in blocked state instead of forwarding state, preventing seamless client movement. Workaround: Reduce the MAC-age timer to minimize the impact. or configure AP ports in device-mode instead of client-mode: interface 1/1/X port-access client-limit 1 port-access client-mode device.

description: Group-Based Policy (GBP) role binding is not supported in combination with 802.1X or MAC Authentication on the same interface. Configuring these features together may result in unpredictable authentication and policy enforcement behavior. This software release does not provide validation or warnings for this configuration conflict, and administrators must avoid this combination manually. Scenario: This issue may occur when attempting to enable policy validation for the 8325 platform, particularly in configurations involving subnet-based GBP. The AAA configuration will not apply to ports enabled for subnet-based GBP, leading to potential access policy conflicts.

description: Symptom: The VSX daemon may become unresponsive or hang due to a deadlock condition.
